title: lupinus arcticus subsp. subalpinus
intro: Lupinus arcticus subsp. subalpinus, also known as the Arctic lupine, is a subspecies of lupine native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is a perennial plant with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its high content of isoflavones, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lupinus arcticus subsp. subalpinus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes and other conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a medicinal treatment.
